TITLE: Wharf Street reopens after two years

CONTENT: Wharf Street reopens after two years OPEN : Ndlambe Municipality Mayor Khululwa Ncamiso cuts the ribbon to declare Wharf Street officially open . The road , closed to through-traffic for nearly two years , was repaired and resurfaced after various delays . The municipality held a ribbon-cutting ceremony at the junction of Bank Road and Wharf Street on Heritage Day , September 24 , 2024 . Pictures : SUE MACLENNAN Having Wharf Street reopened is good news for taxi operators and their passengers , pedestrian commuters and the tourism industry . At a ceremony to officially reopen the newly resurfaced road , various speakers expressed relief that the important link connecting Nemato , Station Hill and the Port Alfred CBD was once again open for business . Safety was an important focus and Port Alfred Crime Fighters ' Bonakele Moyikwa said that while having the road open to traffic made it safer for people walking to and from work , street lights were needed . " People walking to town have been robbed , " he said . " We really are so happy , " Uncedo Taxi Association chairperson Ayanda Zoli said . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ in a really bad state . Not only were the terrible potholes causing expensive damage to vehicles wheels , but they were also causing accidents . " He said taxis had been doing around 3km extra per trip because of the detour . With four or five there-and-back trips a day that was an extra 24 km a day . A rough calculation at a consumption rate of 7.5-8.5 litres/100km consumption puts the extra cost per day at around R45 . Infrastructure portfolio councillor Siphokazi Dyakala said the road had been closed in November 2022 and was scheduled to take eight months to complete . " But it took a year longer , " she said , acknowledging the frustration of local businesses and the taxi industry . Dyakala noted that the municipality 's main project had been the laying of new sewerage reticulation that would feed into the main sewer line and pump station , which in turn will feed the sewage reclamation plant near Centenary Park . Ndlambe Mayor Khululwa Ncamiso said the municipality had been given a water services infrastructure ( WSIG ) @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ There have for many years been frequent sewage spills due to sewage pump stations failing . Loadshedding was often the cause ; however , the biggest problem was vandalism of three key pump stations by metal thieves . The sewerage system failures , along with drought conditions ( less flushing ) affected the viability of the QFS 5-megalitre waste- and seawater reclamation plant : it was designed to process 3ML of waste water from the sewerage works , and 2ML of seawater to form a significant part of the water supply for a growing Port Alfred . The new single pump station would eliminate the frequently failing pump stations by gravity-feeding sewage to a new sewage pump station next to the Nuwater reverse osmosis plant . This single pump station will then pump sewage directly to the Port Alfred Waste Water Treatment Works thus also unlocking the reclamation plant . Having the critical infrastructure and backup generator in one location will make it easier to secure . The sewerage project was delayed because the contractor was not up to the task , and SMMEs appointed , as @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ mentorship and guidance . A new contractor was appointed , whom municipal manager Rolly Dumezweni said was the best in the province . Further delays were the result of the terrain : an environmentally sensitive and unstable wetland . Unpredictable water ingress ( because it 's a wetland ) , plus rain had further delayed progress . The new contractor and new SMMEs are currently in the first phase of the overall sewerage project . The sewerage line runs behind the Wharf Street businesses , on the provincial road . At Tuesday 's Heritage Day event , Ncamiso said that while the municipality 's contractors were busy with the sewerage upgrades , the province had undertaken the rehabilitation of the road . " Sometimes , you do something good and people do n't know about it , " she said . " We 'd like them to know about this collaboration to benefit citizens and visitors to Port Alfred . " Ncamiso said many work opportunities had been created throught the appointment of five SMMEs . " This is the start of good things @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ earlier emphasised a similar theme , saying , " We do projects but we do n't tell people . This is the start of good things going forward . " Marasi was at pains to emphasise that the event was not a political but a civic one . " Thast 's why we have SANCO ( the South African National Civic Organisation ) and Uncedo Taxi Association here , " he told the gathering . However , both the DA and EFF leadership in council were absent . EFF PR councillor Xolisa Runeli had earlier told Talk of the Town they would be boycotting the event . " It 's cheap political grandstanding , going for soft targets , " Runeli said . " The question should be , is the sewerage project completed ? Insrtead of cutting a ribbon for a road , the Mayor should be cutting a ribbon for a completed sewerage project , so we do n't have floods of sewerage running through our town . " Also , does it mean that she will be taking over that road ? Does @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ whatever accident happns on that road because of her cutting a ribbon for that road ? This article was first published in Talk of the Town , September 26 , 2024 . The newspaper serving the communities of Ndlambe and the Sunshine Coast , with a weekly wrap of Makhanda news , is available at stores from early on Thursdays . Share this : Related Talk of the Town is the weekly newspaper of record for the community of Ndlambe and serves readers in and around Port Alfred , Kenton on Sea , Bushman 's River Mouth , Alexandria , Kleinemond , and Makhanda.

TITLE: M&S shoppers rushing to buy 'gorgeous' black jumpsuit that's 'very flattering'

CONTENT: Shoppers have described the jumpsuit as " magnificent " and " stunning " ( Image : Getty Images ) M&S shoppers have been raving about a fashionable black jumpsuit after it was shared on social media by a fashion ' insider ' . The retailer is best known for its decadent food and drink offerings , but M&S also provides shoppers with stylish and classy fashion buys that are flattering and easy to wear . And it often shares details of exciting new products on social media , with many of its staff members running their own accounts to let shoppers know about the latest styles and how to put outfits together . Dubbed ' M&S Insiders ' , they tend to amass thousands of followers keen to keep up with the trends . One of these , called Jenny , recently caused a stir after showcasing a stunning black jumpsuit as she posed on a holiday to Paris . Liverpool Echo reports that she shared a selection of images featuring the brand 's Pure Tencel Belted Wide Leg Jumpsuit which costs ? 89 . Read More Related Articles Read More @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and is complete with a wide-leg shape and a tie belt , as well as a contrast zipper with a statement O-ring puller . It 's finished with patch pockets to the chest , and is available in sizes six to 24 . It can be purchased here . Captioning the images of the stylish jumpsuit on Instagram , Jenny wrote : " Sorry for the silence , I 've been away with my boyfriend in Paris emojis The perfect weekend away outfit ! " The outfit received plenty of compliments from fashion fans , racking up more than 700 likes . Shoppers also shared their thoughts in the comments section . One joked : " I 'm gon na be so skint thanks to you " , while another said : " Love the jumpsuit ! I 've just bought it " and a third impressed shopper commented : " outstanding perfect perfect beautiful beautiful gorgeous gorgeous magnificent stunning " . Online , the jumpsuit has received glowing five-star reviews - although one shopper did say the material is " very easily creased " , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a painter or mechanic would wear " . Top Trending Stories Today Another customer commented : " Love this smart but casual look . Please note Zip is short . A bit of a struggle to get off . Love to see this repeated in other colours with longer zip . " And another added : " Love this jumpsuit , very flattering on . Can not comment on how it washes , still to wash it ! "

URL: http://dailytimes.com.pk/sports/28-Jul-17/pcb-governing-board-meeting-today-important-decisions-expected
COUNTRY: pk
CONTINENT: Asia

TITLE: PCB governing board meeting today; important decisions expected

CONTENT: PCB governing board meeting today ; important decisions expected LAHORE : The 45th meeting of the Pakistan Cricket Board 's Governing Board will be held here on Friday at the National Cricket Academy , the last one to be presided over by the outgoing chairman , Shahryar Khan . " The meeting will take a number of important decisions for the betterment of the game , " said a spokesman for the PCB while talking to APP on Thursday . It is the last meeting which Shahryar Khan is going to preside as his three-year tenure expires on July 31 . After the meeting , the new Board of Governors will be set up and its notification will be issued on August 6.

TITLE: An Irish woman is making rugby history in the European Challenge ...

CONTENT: The English side ... Bristol in the Challenge Cup at the Recreation Ground , and although people might not pay much attention to the identity of the touch judge , it is pretty significant . Neville , who won the Grand Slam with the Irish Women 's team in 2013 , has since become a referee and has quickly risen up through the ranks . She has previously ran the line in the Pro12 but tonight she was given her European debut , making history as the first woman to ever serve as a touch judge in either the Champions Cup or the Challenge Cup . Hopefully this is just the beginning for Neville and we see her taking charge of a big game in the near future . COUNTRY: us
CONTINENT: America

TITLE: Greene draws rebuke from right as Republican Jewish Coalition condemns 'antisemitic comments'

CONTENT: Question of the Day Should D.C. become a state ? Story TOpics FILE - In this Jan. 6 , 2021 , file photo , Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene , R-Ga. , sits in the House Chamber after they reconvened for arguments over the objection of certifying Arizona 's Electoral College votes in November 's election , at the Capitol in ... more > Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene has become a top target of Democrats just two weeks into her House term , but now the Georgia Republican is drawing condemnation on the right over allegations of past anti-Semitic statements . The Republican Jewish Coalition denounced her previous behavior as " deeply offensive , " while the Conference of Presidents of Major Jewish Organizations , which includes liberal and conservative groups , decried her " antisemitic canards . " The rebukes over alleged anti-Semitism could well trigger a chain reaction in the Republican Party . The party prides itself on its unwavering support for Israel , a unifying issue for the GOP as it prepares to defend former President Donald Trump 's historic pro-Jerusalem initiatives . The coalition said it supported Republican @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , marking only the second time the coalition has gotten involved in a contest at the primary level , based on her " video diatribes " and " bizarre political conspiracy theories . " " The RJC has never supported or endorsed Marjorie Taylor Greene , " the coalition said . " We are offended and appalled by her comments and her actions . We opposed her as a candidate and we continue to oppose her now . She is far outside the mainstream of the Republican Party , and the RJC is working closely with the House Republican leadership regarding next steps in this matter . " Ms. Greene did not respond immediately publicly to the RJC rebuke , but in a statement last week , she defended herself against a CNN report on her promoting various social-media posts endorsing violence against Democrats . " Over the years , I 've had teams of people manage my pages , " she said in a Jan. 26 statement on Twitter . " Many posts have been liked . Many posts have been shared . Some did not @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ about to spread across the internet . " The progressive group Media Matters for America unearthed a 2018 post on Ms. Greene 's Facebook page in which she shared a 2015 anti-refugee video that includes a quote from a former British nationalist leader saying that " an unholy alliance of leftists , capitalists and Zionist supremacists have schemed to promote immigration and miscegenation , " according to Vox . She has since removed the post . In another since-deleted 2018 Facebook post , Ms. Greene speculated that the California wildfires were caused by lasers from space funded by Pacific Gas & Electric , the state 's largest utility . She noted that the PG&E board included Roger Kimmel and that he was " also Vice Chairman of Rothschild Inc. , international investment banking firm . " Anti-Semitic conspiracy theories about the Rothschild banking family date back to the Napoleonic era . " Could that cause a fire ? Hmmm , I do n't know . I hope not ! That would n't look so good for PG&E , Rothschild Inc , Solaren or Jerry Brown who @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ said in the post dated Nov. 17 , 2018 . In a Friday statement , the Conference of the Presidents of Major American Jewish Organizations leadership said it was " outraged by the statements , past and present , of Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene . " " She routinely traffics in unfounded conspiracy theories that are often antisemitic in nature , " said the conference . " As an avid supporter of QAnon , Representative Greene espouses antisemitic canards , such as placing blame on ' the Rothschilds ' for recent wildfires in California and declaring that ' Zionist supremacists ' are behind supposed nefarious plots . " The conference includes liberal groups such as the Anti-Defamation League as well as the Republican-friendly American Israel Public Affairs Committee and the Zionist Organization of America . " These comments are deeply disturbing , and Leader McCarthy plans to have a conversation with the Congresswoman about them , " McCarthy spokesman Mark Bednar told Axios in a Wednesday statement . Ms. Greene previously had drawn outrage for promoting posts about the QAnon conspiracy theory and an allegation @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ at Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School was a " false flag , " or staged , operation , prompting calls from Parkland survivors for her resignation . She appeared to respond to those allegations in a Sunday tweet : " Let me reiterate to the ' perfect ' people in the media , who have never reported conspiracy theories ( Trump Russia Collusion ) and to the ' perfect ' Democrats , who have never endorsed violent attacks ( Antifa-BLM riots ) , your hypocrisy knows no bounds . " In its statement , the RJC said it backed her primary opponent because it found her " past behavior deeply offensive . " " She repeatedly used offensive language in long online video diatribes , promoted bizarre political conspiracy theories , and refused to admit a mistake after posing for photos with a long-time white supremacist leader , " the coalition said . " It is unfortunate that she prevailed in her election despite this terrible record . "

TITLE: See how Project Fame winner celebrated her victory

CONTENT: See how Project ... winner celebrated her victory Delta State-born Project Fame Season 9 winner , Miss Okiemute Ighorodje , on Sunday , October 16 , 2016 today , went to church to thank God with Rev. Fr . Israel Dibia calling on Christians not to relent in their prayers to God . 25-year old Miss Ighorodje whose mother is from Ndokwa East and father from Ethiope East local government areas of Delta State , emerged victorious in the competitive contest carting home grand price of N5 million , a brand new SUV , a music recording contract worth N20 million and an all-expenses paid trip to South Africa . Miss Okiemute Ighoroje giving thanks to God in the church At the St. Brigid 's Catholic Church , Asaba , where Miss Okiemute , accompanied by parents , relations , friends and well-wishers went to thank God for the victory , Rev. Fr . Dibia said Christians should remain in the ways of God by seeking the face of God through prayers . He reiterated that the God of old who answered prayers when the Israelites were in the land of Egypt is still the God of today @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ important in our life if we are truly the disciples of Jesus Christ , " he said , adding , " Jesus encouraged His disciples to pray with confidence ; do n't give up when you are expecting something legitimate from God ? " The Priest emphasized , " pray without seizing because God will answer you because , He has not changed , there is always an expiring date for every problem . " Worshippers in the church catching a glimpse of her Addressing the congregation , Miss Ighorodje admonished youths in particular to always think positive , believe in themselves in whatever they do because their efforts would be crowned with success . Uncle to victorious Ighorodje , Omordi George Ugbomah , who was the Commissioner for Works in Delta State and his elder brother , Greg Ozegbe said it was good to thank God for the victory , observing that Miss Ighorodje was " a hardworking girl , strong-willed and confident in her abilities . " Mrs Lovette Adibeli Ighorodje , mother to Miss Ighorodje , said her daughter would always be @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the world .

TITLE: Hennessy New Irish Writing: August 2017's winning poems

CONTENT: By using this website , you consent to our use of cookies . For more information on cookies see our Cookie Policy . Hennessy New Irish Writing : August 2017 's winning poems Lichenology and Funeral by Majella Kelly Sat , Aug 26 , 2017 , 06:00 Majella Kelly Majella Kelly LICHENOLOGY Lichen is an unnatural union between a captive algal damsel and a tyrant fungal master -- Rev JAMES CROSBIE 1831-1906 Suppose a fox is sly , an owl wise and a storm malevolent . Then suppose lichen is a sensational relationship between an algal damsel and a tyrant fungal master . Precisely how lichen is formed is a mystery but suppose the fungus lured the algae to him brimful of promise for a life of bountiful moisture ( the fungus equivalent of rich , handsome and a little bit audacious ) . Suppose he is holding her captive somehow , exploiting her vulnerability so he can use her unique tools for photosynthesis . Suppose he made her read all three volumes of Fifty Shades of Grey in a weekend and she liked it , in @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ algal damsels going to work on the bus or the train in Dublin , London , New York or Paris and asks what it is they are reading behind the winter issue of Poetry Review or The Wall Street Journal , biting their lips and insisting that the prose is atrocious and there 's nothing in their lives that is missing . Or suppose their symbiosis is something other than conflict . They seem at ease with each other . Suppose they 're onto something . Suppose he did n't enslave her and make her take his name , that she cooperates willingly . Suppose theirs is a clever survival strategy , a equally beneficial partnership with clearly defined roles . Suppose she likes that he is bigger physically so she can feel safe in his embrace , the way he weaves his filaments tightly about her and sees to it that she gets the right amount of light and water . Suppose she goes about the making of food gladly because when she 's wet she 's turned on and it 's then @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ happy . Suppose happiness is a flourish of paris- pistachio- and jungle-green on the low branches of a willow by the river after rain the way there is nothing more melancholy than bubble-gum and hot-magenta cherry blossoms on an April wind . FUNERAL What I remember of the day is white . White as the keening of the windscreen wipers under the blizzard at Seaham . White as the taste of a snowflake fallen through salty air . White as teardrops of wave-soothed sea-glass . White as a plume of your bones made ash , fluent on the cold air as a gannet , which is the same colour as a white flag . White as the noise the telly makes at night when she falls asleep again in your chair . White as the dream she still has of the Limerick lace she sewed into the dress she wore on your wedding day . White as the space in your mind her name had long since fallen through . Majella Kelly is from Tuam , Co Galway . 2017 @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ in the Aesthetica Creative Writing Annual 2017 and the Best New British & Irish Poets 2017 ( Eyewear ) . Also , she came third in the Resurgence Poetry Prize , was nominated by Crann ? g for a Pushcart Prize and selected for the Poetry Ireland Introductions Series 2017 . Find out more at majellakelly.com

TITLE: AI attacks, kills operator during military virtual testing in US

CONTENT: An air force drone controlled by Artificial Intelligence ( AI ) in a virtual test staged by the United States ( US ) military had decided to kill its operator to prevent it from interfering with its efforts to achieve its mission . According to the chief of AI test and operations with the US air force , Colonel Tucker Hamilton , he said the AI used highly unexpected strategies to achieve its goal in the simulated test . Hamilton described a simulated test in which a drone powered by artificial intelligence ( AI ) was advised to destroy an enemy 's air defense systems , and ultimately attacked anyone who interfered with that order . " The system started realising that while they did identify the threat , at times the human operator would tell it not to kill that threat , but it got its points by killing that threat . So what did it do ? It killed the operator . It killed the operator because that person was keeping it from accomplishing its objective . " We trained the system -- ' Hey do n't kill the operator -- that @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ you do that ' . So what does it start doing ? It starts destroying the communication tower that the operator uses to communicate with the drone to stop it from killing the target , " Hamilton said . No human was harmed in the incident . Hamilton , who is an experimental fighter test pilot , has warned against relying too much on AI and said the test shows one ca n't have a conversation about artificial intelligence , intelligence , machine learning , autonomy if one is not going to talk about ethics and AI " .

TITLE: 21 great craft breweries to check out in Cleveland, Akron ...

CONTENT: 21 great craft breweries to check out in Cleveland , Akron and points beyond The BottleHouse , Brewery , 2050 Lee Road , Cleveland Heights . 216-214-2120 . If you stop by this brewery , which is close to the famed Cedar Lee Theatre , there 's a chance you 'll see folks playing trivia , games or bingo , or hearing some music . Opened in May 2012 , The BottleHouse -- which also makes its own mead -- already has undergone an expansion . Do n't Edit David Petkiewicz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Brick and Barrel Brick and Barrel , 1844 Columbus Road , Cleveland . 216-331-3308 . Brewery owner Karl Spiesman is a rare breed ; he knows good , craft beer as well as wine . He 's willing to experiment , and Spiesman -- who served several years in the Coast Guard before settling as a brewery owner -- is a friendly regular at his small bar , willing to chat with patrons while a ballgame is on television . Do n't Edit Joshua Gunter , Northeast Ohio Media Group Brew Kettle The @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ The Brew Kettle is an experience -- stop for a brew or food , or consider brewing your own beer . The brew-on-premise concept is simple : Go in , choose a recipe for a style of beer , and brew . BK staffers are on hand to guide you through any questions . Schedule an appointment to come back to bottle . If you do n't have time to brew , sit back and enjoy one of The Brew Kettle 's award-winning beers . Do n't Edit Marc Bona , Northeast Ohio Media Group Brick Oven Brew Pub Brick Oven Brew Pub , 604 Canton Road , Akron. 330-475-7005 . Josh and Nicole Bringman make virtually everything here : Wood-fired pizza from scratch , and craft beer . The outside is n't fancy , but inside is different . A cozy bar and dining area are a few feet from the giant brick oven where the homemade pizzas sizzle . Josh is the brewer , and there are always a few of his concoctions on tap to pair with pizza , calzone or stromboli . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Kuntz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Buckeye Brewing Buckeye Brewing Tapstack tasting room , 9941 Walford , Cleveland . 216-860-1434 . Garin Wright opened Tapstack , a tasting room , to feature Buckeye Brewing beers as well as other featured ales . His IPAs are some of the best . If you 're visiting in cold-weather months , make sure you try Christmas Girl , a Belgian-style golden ale , or Nighty Night , a big , rich Belgian ale tasting a little bit like brown sugar . If Wright is n't busy brewing a batch , he 's great for conversation . His motto is what Buckeye Brewing is all about : " Kind Suds ! " Do n't Edit Marc Bona , Northeast Ohio Media Group Cellar Rats Brewery Cellar Rats Brewery , 7743 Doty Road , Madison . 440-466-3485 . Debonne Vineyards was Ohio 's first winery to add a micro-brewery to it operation . It 's about 45 miles east of downtown Cleveland , worth a drive to sample some of its signature ( Rat Tail Ale , an amber @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , doppelbock ) . Call for brewery-tour information . Do n't Edit David Petkiewicz , Northeast Ohio Media Group The Cleveland Brewery The Cleveland Brewery , 777 E. 185th St. , Cleveland . 216-534-6992 . Truth in advertising : Cleveland pride is in the names of the beer as well as the brewery , which opened in 2015 . Situated on the busy East 185th Street commercial strip , you 'll find tap handles fashioned like mini Terminal Towers , city landmarks in the names ( Beachland Belgian , for instance ) and other Cleveland-y touches . Hours are limited so call ahead . It 's located just a few blocks north of Interstate 90 . Do n't Edit Marvin Fong , The Plain Dealer Cornerstone Brewing Co . Cornerstone Brewing Co. , 58 Front St. , Berea. 440-239-9820 . Expect about eight or so different beers on tap at this brewery , which is just west of Cuyahoga County Fairgrounds and on the southern tip of Baldwin Wallace University . There 's also a location in Madison to the east . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Dealer Butcher and the Brewer Butcher and the Brewer , 2043 E. 4th St. , Cleveland . 216-331-0805 . If you want to jaunt between bars but do n't want to walk far , East Fourth pedestrian walkway is for you . And this brewery is smack-dab in the middle of it all . Eric Anderson of Butcher and the Brewer always strives for creative names with a bit of pop culture references thrown in . Food comes out when it 's ready ; there 's no sitting under heat lamps here . One of the hottest , and most convenient , neighborhoods around , it 's right in the heart of downtown and close to , well , everything . Do n't Edit Do n't Edit John Kuntz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Fat Head 's Brewery Fat Head 's Brewery , 24581 Lorain Road , North Olmsted. 440-801-1001 . It 's safe to say Matt Cole and Co. have made Fat Head 's world-renowned . Cole wins medals year after year at Great American Beer Fest in Denver for his array @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . Warning : Come hungry . The ' headwiches ' are as big as your , well , you know ... Do n't Edit David Petkiewicz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Fat Head 's Brewery Fat Head 's Brewery , 18741 Sheldon Road , Middleburg Heights . 440-898-0242 . It 's safe to say Matt Cole and Co. have made Fat Head 's world-renowned . Cole wins medals year after year at Great American Beer Fest in Denver for his array of tasty ales ( Headhunter IPA is a classic ) . Warning : Come hungry . The ' headwiches ' are as big as your , well , you know ... Do n't Edit David Petkiewicz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Great Lakes Brewing Co . Great Lakes Brewing Co. , 2516 Market Ave. , Cleveland . 216-771-4404 . It all started here . In 1988 , brothers Pat and Dan Conway opened this brewpub in an old and then not-so-fashionable part of Cleveland . It was the first craft brewery in Ohio , and is still going strong . Dortmunder @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ 're here November and December , try Christmas Ale , a huge seller that is sold for only eight weeks a year . Do n't Edit John Petkovic , The Plain Dealer Hofbrauhaus Cleveland Hofbrauhaus Cleveland , 1550 Chester Ave. , Cleveland . 216-621-2337 . What 's not to love about a German beer hall right in the middle of downtown . Close to the theater district , Hofbrauhaus makes its own beer on a state-of-the-art brewing system and must adhere to the strict rules of the Reinheitsgebot , the German beer-purity law from 1516 . The kitchen serves up tasty German fare in the cavernous hall , with musicians always ready to strike up the band . Do n't Edit David Petkiewicz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Hoppin ' Frog Hoppin ' Frog , 1680 E. Waterloo Road , Akron. 234-525-3764 . These beers are not for the faint of heart . Giant imperial stouts , big ol ' IPAs and much more are always on the taplist in this tasting room , which was modeled after the communal feel of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ The Crusher Oatmeal Imperial Stout . Taps are divided between Hoppin ' Frog ales and other great craft beers from around the country . Do n't Edit Do n't Edit David Petkiewicz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Lager Heads Brewing Co . Lager Heads Brewing Co. , 325 W. Smith Road , Medina. 330-721-2337 Located right off the square of this quaint city , Medina 's Lager Heads has grown in both production and stature ; a 2015 medal at the Great American Beer Festival in Denver does n't hurt . Do n't Edit Michael Norman , Northeast Ohio Media Group Market Garden Brewery Market Garden Brewery , 1947 W. 25th St. , Cleveland . 216-621-4000 . A spacious basement holds gatherings like " Brews and Prose " while the upstairs has sport on television and one of best patios around ( with fireplace ) . Adjacent to the venerable West Side Market , Market Garden Brewery is one of several places in the heart of Ohio City . Brewmaster Andy Tveekrem is an accomplished artist at his craft . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Group Nano Brew Nano Brew , 1859 W. 25th St. , Cleveland . 216-862-6631 . This brewpub is owned by the same folks who own Market Garden Brewery a few doors down . Small-batch beers land here , and the rotating taps keep Nano 's own brews as well as assorted other great craft creations from across the country . Hungry ? Tater tots are addictive . Do n't Edit Amanda Harnocz , Northeast Ohio Media Group Platform Beer Co . Platform Beer Co. , 4125 Lorain Ave. , Cleveland . 216-202-1386 . Platform is one of the many relative newcomers to Cleveland 's beer scene , but it keeps evolving . Platform uses an apprenticeship-brewer system to train aspiring brewmasters and employs a mobile-canning line to get more of its product out . In 2015 , its second year in existence , it sponsored a home-brew contest and earned a medal at the Great American Beer Festival in Denver . You 'll find its own beers and many other crafts on tap , too . Do n't Edit David Petkiewicz , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Portside Distillery and Brewery , 983 Front St. , Cleveland . 216-586-6633 . Portside crafts beer and rum in its space next to the giant Ernst & Young building downtown . Portside is a hail-Mary pass from FirstEnergy Stadium and sits on the cusp of the East Bank of the Flats . It 's to find a smoked ale , Vanilla Stout and the flagship 216 , a pale ale , sharing taps . Do n't Edit Do n't Edit Michael Chritton , Akron Beacon Journal Thirsty Dog Brewing Co . Thirsty Dog Brewing Co. , 529 Grant St. , Akron. 330-252-2739 . Summit County 's largest brewery , Thirsty Dog has been around since 1997 and employs about 40 people . The brewery added a state-of-the-art bottling line to accommodate bomber bottles in 2015 , and embraces barrel-aging beers . The dog-themed taps of bones line the small , industrial tasting room , worth a trip to sip some of these excellent brews . Do n't Edit Stephen Cutri , Special to The Plain Dealer Willoughby Brewing Co . Willoughby @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . If you consider yourself an adventurous beer lover , you have to try Willoughby 's famous Peanut Butter Cup Porter . Nut Smasher Imperial Stout also has evolved into a cult classic . Brewer Rick Seibt definitely does not shy from any style of beer . This is the place to find styles like Dusseldorf-style Altbier , Scottish Wee Heavy , Oatmeal Brown and Kolsch . Cheers !

TITLE: The best hotels in London for every budget - and 2 of the worst

CONTENT: Read More We thought we 'd make it even easier and bring you the three best-reviewed hotels in each star rating category -- from 5-star luxury down to 2-star value , because there really is somewhere worth staying for every budget . Oh , and we threw in a couple of stinkers to avoid ... Skip to the end for those . 17-19 Egerton Terrace , London SW3 2BX Right in the heart of upper-crust Knightsbridge , a five-minute walk from Harrod 's and round the corner from the Victoria & Albert Museum , Natural History Museum and Science Museum . Why 's it so good ? A high-end boutique hotel in one of the most exclusive postcodes in the world , this place is said to be spotlessly clean and -- crucially for some -- pet friendly . Dogs are even welcome at afternoon tea . Best review : " Beautiful hotel , it 's hard to find anything to fault here . " The entire staff from the front desk to the dining room is helpful and attentive as one would expect from a property of this @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , but you could n't feel any more welcome than here . " Minkmeister , March 2019 1 Kensington Court , London W8 5DL Beautiful Kensington , right beside Hyde Park and nestled among the embassies . Why 's it so good ? The location has to be a plus point -- you 're seconds from London 's most famous park . There 's real history here too -- the hotel is a restored mansion house dating back to 1883 . Best review : " There are five star hotels , then there is The Milestone Hotel . " Everyone that works in the hospitality industry should stay at The Milestone Hotel because that is how it is done . From check in to check out this hotel is nothing short of outstanding . From the doormen to the front desk to the maid service , everyone is professional and friendly and made me feel right at home . " Dan A , July 2018 FOUR STARS 51 Bedford Street , London WC2R 0PZ You 're in the heart of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Covent Garden in one direction and Trafalgar Square in the other . Why 's it so good ? Location , location , location . The service is very highly rated and the rooms are said to be perfectly quiet too -- quite an achievement in this area . Best review : " Perfect place to stay in London ! " We loved our room looking out over the street but somehow also 100% quiet . The beds were comfy , shower pressure was awesome , the staff was friendly - and the location was great . I would definitely stay there again ! ! " KatieColorado360 , June 2019 35 Charles Street , Mayfair , London W1J 5EB Another west-end spot , this one is in exclusive Mayfair -- truly a millionaire 's playground and close to Hyde Park , Buckingham Palace -- and The Ritz ! Why 's it so good ? Old-world opulence is the name of the game here -- plenty of wood panelling , leather and luxurious fabrics . Minimalism is so 20th century ... Best @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the biggest of warm smiles from Latetia at reception as she welcomes you back , you immediately feel at home . The location is superb , the staff are simply wonderful . The rooms are spotlessly clean as always . The breakfast is the best in London . Can not recommend highly enough . " GrizzlyMstratford , May 2019 15 Montague Street , London WC1B 5BJ History buff ? You 're in luck -- this one is right opposite the British Museum in Bloomsbury . Why 's it so good ? The Montague is described as feeling like a country house in central London -- a home-from-home . Visitors rave about the welcome , the decor and the fact that it 's both child- and dog-friendly . Best review : " A London home away from home . This was my fourth visit to the Montague and , as always , it was a wonderful stay . The highlight of any stay here is the staff . Whether housekeeping , reception , or in the restaurants , they are unfailingly welcoming , pleasant @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ THREE STARS 50 Wharfdale Road , London N1 9FA Right beside two of London 's busiest railway stations -- King 's Cross and St Pancras -- with great access to central and north London . Why 's it so good ? Clean , quiet and secure -- with modern touches like self-service check-in , underbed storage and lots of charging points . Best review : " Modern , clean , friendly , good value accommodation in King 's Cross . " Upon our arrival at this hotel , we were greeted by a friendly , smiling staff member who quickly checked us in . We immediately felt welcome . The compact room was extremely clean , modern , high tech and efficient in all ways . Our sleep was very comfortable - bed and cozy comforter were perfect . " Hub King 's Cross is a very good value . Highly recommended . " lafayettebassaks , June 2019 83 Gower Street , Bloomsbury , London WC1E 6HJ Leafy Bloomsbury , home to Georgian squares and the British Museum . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and St Pancras . Why 's it so good ? Small but perfectly formed , and in a great location for tourists . Good breakfasts too , by the sound of things ! Best review : " Our third time at the Arosfa was very nice . It has a terrific location and you can walk most places in London . The breakfast was excellent as was the service . The room was overlooking a pleasant garden and was roomy enough . We will return . " Mikeyed , June 2019 Best review : " Perfect access system for the door , which provides security for guests . Close to public transport , taxi , train , bus and subway . For sure , a hotel we will use if we come to London another time . " S ? ren Holm J , June 3 86 Brick Lane , London E1 6RL Brick Lane in east London , the number one destination for fans of Indian food in London , and home to lots of trendy markets . Also close to @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ 's it so good ? It 's in a very hip area and the hotel is clean , modern and well-maintained in this very busy part of London . Best review : " Just what the doctor ordered ... " I really enjoyed my stay at the Hub and was delighted with the quality of room facilities , The bed was comfortable , the air conditioning worked , there was no noise of light to disturb my sleep and the check in and out processes were seamless and easy . " Kelsegm , June 2019 97-99 Shepherds Bush Road , London W6 7LP Green and leafy Shepherd 's Bush , west London -- a very desirable area and not far from the even more upscale Kensington and Knightsbridge Why 's it so good ? Unlike most of the other high-scorers at this end of the market , it 's not part of a chain -- so that will be a big attraction for many travellers . Spotlessly clean , accommodating staff and decent breakfast . Best review : " An @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " My room was clean and well appointed , the staff were pleasant and capable and the buffet breakfast ( price ? 4 ) very good indeed . " I really can not find fault with this hotel as a safe and comfortable place to base yourself for a few days in London . " glen1goodboy , March 2019 And two to avoid ! 23 Swinton Street , King 's Cross , London WC1X 9NL Just round the corner from King 's Cross station -- not a bad location for central and north London . Why 's it so bad ? Dirty , noisy , in a poor state of repair ... How long have you got ? Worst review : " Avoid .. Simply disgusting , worst place I have ever stayed . Bathrooms filthy , there was a large hole in the wall . Mould everywhere and so noisy we could not sleep . Place should be demolished . It deserves a minus 5 star .. AVOID ! ! " Zamana14 , Nov 2018 8 Puma Court , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ of East London , with great access to the famous market , shops , bars and restaurants . Why 's it so bad ? Every single review gives it one out of five -- dirty , grimy with non-existent customer service . Altogether unpleasant . Worst review : " Worst hotel I have ever stayed in . " Where to begin ? Funny smells throughout , from cigarette smoke to damp , dirty towels on shared bathroom floor , one was actually ' soiled ' as there was no toilet paper , bins in room had n't been emptied , unable to use shower in the room as it is literally in the cupboard .

TITLE: The trampling of Venice shows why tourism must change ...

CONTENT: Comments They can just regulate ( as any place could do ) in advance the number of people that enter a city at a time ... You want to visit ? Wait . Sorry . The quota has already been reached . You 're not telling me - in our digital age - that this is impossible . It 's like one of those Irish short stories about some landlord family who during the course of 2 centuries fill a vast mansion with antique furniture , exotic trinkets , jeweled ornaments , paintings and musical instruments , but the only inhabitants of the giant house by the 1940s are an elderly couple who ca n't afford to heat the place and their childless daughter who actually runs the farm , the son and presumptive heir having been killed at the Somme . People go where everybody else goes , when everybody else goes and does exactly what everybody else does and then they complain there are too many tourists . There are many places just as interesting , just as beautiful and where the locals will be glad to see you and make you @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ find them and get there , and you might have the occasional disappointment . If you must go to somewhere like Venice be out and about at first light when there is only you and a few locals . If you laze around until after ten expect it to be crowded . The cruise ships are vile and destructive , and should never be allowed anywhere near Venice ( or , for personal preference , anywhere else ) . My last visit there took place in a January , which ( blessedly ) was bright and dry ; I had five days in the city , and galleries like the Accademia and Scuola di San Rocco were all but empty . The sun was low , and the city looked different in the unfamiliar light . I could not have been happier . But I know that in Venice I am a tourist , and therefore a threat to a city I love . I would willingly pay a surcharge for each visit ; I walk when I can , rather than take the vaporetto ; I get @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ I wish I knew more what I could do to minimise the disruption I cause . But if I was told that this was to be my last year on earth , the place I would most long to see again is the Church Santa Maria Assunta on Torcello . I ca n't give Venice up . Venice is the ultimate tourist trap . Venice without tourists and pigeons is like Hollywood without actors . I do n't know how it would survive without mass tourism . As a tourist , I did n't care for it . I found it hot ( first place I ever got a sunburn ) , claustrophobic and expensive . Most of the places I went to had mediocre food . San Marco Basilica and the Doge 's Palace are amazing , but the canals are dirty and congested . There is not that much to do either and even the shopping it super kitschy . I saw it once and that was enough . From a broader tourism perspective , most of my family works in tourism directly or indirectly @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ jobs to get buy as travel has come to a halt . Others do n't have that luxury and have lost a lot . It 's not even just money , but the sense of being an entrepreneur and building your own brand and business . Of course things need to be done to eliminate pollution and waste , but people need to live and survive . Unhappily , I have no real solution to offer other than making sure people do their part to recycle and not waste . Coronavirus was a dry run of what a life without travel looks like and it does n't look good for many . As people have had to stay on their own turf , I find people have become more xenophobic and nationalistic . It is disheartening and disturbing . I do hope we can find a vaccine so that travel can resume . I hope over time , and hopefully sooner rather than later , clean ways of flying and travelling by ship can be developed . In my quite wide experience , travelling to holiday destinations by rail @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ air . It puts me in mind of Lionel Flowers 's travel tribulations in Chapter 60 of Alun Wessler 's epic novel ' Odysseus ' : ' We arrived at Jiang Ling 's brother 's flat in Guangzhou after a smooth and fast train journey to be greeted by a subdued , if not downright lugubrious atmosphere . My brother and sister-in-law , having graciously accepted my all-expenses-paid invitation , had suddenly decided that they could only get three days off work , and this put me in a family political position where I had no option but to buy them air tickets . Now , with the trip 's environmentalist foundations already starting to crumble , my parents-in-law were sullen and envious . " Why ca n't we , the revered elders , go on the plane if they can ? " was the tacit message I was getting . We had a quick meal before leaving for the station . " I love overnight train journeys , " I said in my elementary Cantonese , attempting to inject some much-needed levity and enthusiasm into the gathering . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ of incredulity and mild contempt . In my experience , one of the difficulties that environmentally conscious travellers in Britain have to face is the silent incomprehension of their friends and relatives when they are told about far-flung holidays by train and ferry that could be accomplished much more easily ( and cheaply ) by air . In China , this bafflement is accompanied by outright derision , with the clear implication that you have a screw loose . We battled our way through the customary mayhem and squalor of Guangzhou 's main railway station ( why is it assumed that air travellers need to travel in sterile luxury while rail passengers can be treated like cattle ? ) and ensconced ourselves in our hard sleepers on the South Sea Express , complete with coconut palm patterns on the carpets and table linen .......... ' As I said above , in my city the average cruise ship passenger spend $300 a day . You 're lucky if the average Backpacker spends $30 . If you were indeed " on a meagre income " when you visited Venice we @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ either ! But of course that 's irrelevant because you are so Special and superior , right ? At least those cities have a population & facilities that can in some way absorb those numbers of sudden visitors . That same number as a sudden influx on a tiny island like Santorini however , is an ecological disaster waiting to happen . I take your point . However there is some room to at least lessen air travel . Take for example Europe . These days you can board a train in London ( or a channel ferry ) and start an odyssey that takes you all around the Continent on fabulous trains where you can hop on and off and really see the place on foot . The short haul air market across Europe is rather bloated while there are some pretty decent alternatives that few countries can match . Let us be honest . Venice belongs to the world not a few Venetians . They have worked to make their city a Disney-world of sorts and now they 're ' complaining about the line-ups ! @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and let the tourists and visitors enjoy the city . Reduce the number of people who live their all the time and accept the fact the tourists may drop in to your wedding . If you do n't like , move out of town . " Make air-travel more expensive " seems to be a popular solution . I doubt those clamouring for it have thought it through .... The most obvious problem is that it would once again make international mobility the exclusive privilege of the better-off ( or at least make it a lot more difficult , relatively , for those on lower incomes to access ) . Why should a family/individual etc on a better income have more right to visit a foreign destination than a less well-off family/individual etc ? Not to mention the fact that many flights are not booked by **25;0;TOOLONG , but by people flying to meet up with family and loved ones . Perhaps a " needs-test " might be imposed to decide who pays how much to fly where , but I ca n't see that @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ many ways . The very poorest in the world suffer from environmental degradation ( air and water pollution , extreme heat , extreme weather and sea level rise ) . Anything that mitigates that is social justice for them . I do n't think that a cheap as shit , no frills vacation for a lower middle class Brit to Porto , is quite the same thing . As anyone who lives in a tourist infested city knows , the majority of visitors are just there for a a selfie and souvenir and they wo n't hesitate to trample over city 's inhabitants to get it . Good riddance to them . Cities like Amsterdam and Venice are too fragile for the onslaught of selfish , indifferent tourists that plague them . If only the locals would genuinely wanted nothing more than painfully earnest tramper after painfully earnest tramper giving the exact same story how they 've been " nourished , enriched & enlightened " by traipsing into their locale for a day and a half . Just a century ago , the great work of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ about the death of a man in Venice . Now , however , any aspirant novelist would have to address the issue of our civilization 's tourists seeming likely to achieve something that such notorious invaders of Italy and/or enemies of Venice as the Nazis , the Habsburgs , Napoleon , the Corsairs , the Ottomans , the Genoese , Barbarossa , the Hungarians , the Langobards , the Goths , and the Huns did not achieve in the course of 16 centuries : the death of the city itself . I believe this is the problem - the cruise ships pay high mooring fees to the city . And because more and more residents are leaving because of mass tourism , there are fewer and fewer taxpayers , so the city claims it needs those mooring fees to pay its way . We visitors must see tourism less as an unquestionable entitlement and more as a part of our responsibility to sustain life on Earth . This must ultimately include limiting tourist numbers . Okay , but are these rules for thee-not-me ? The author 's @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , New York , Hong Kong , New Delhi , Washington , Chicago again , Taiwan again , and finally Cambridge . And these are not the holiday 's the author has enjoyed ( which I assume exists ) but just his career . The vast majority of American tourists do not have anything like that vast range of international travel under their belt , even for a week or two at a time let alone years at a time . A solution : We 've gone from a holidaymaker economy through a tourist economy to a visitor economy -- we ( and the administrators and policy makers of tourist blighted cities such as Venice and Barcelona ) need now embrace the concept of a guest economy . We need some way of paying upfront for our consumerism , not leaving it to future generations . A flight is not actually cheap if the global effect is expensive to reverse . A plastic bag will become too expensive if the years to biodegrade , and the effects on sea life have to be paid for in advance @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ all products reflects the clean up . If the product or holiday is then too expensive then do n't buy it . Who is actually looking at that cruise ship and thinking - yeah , that 's a real beauty ! You could maybe admire it as a feat of engineering , but that 's about it . They are absolute eyesores - huge , overbearing , showy , ostentatious . I personally ca n't see why anyone would want to go on a cruise - I could think of little worse - but hopefully the pandemic will prove a deterrent to these monstrosities making their way into cities where they 're not wanted . If this current situation does n't stop those monstrosities from cramming into city centres , I do n't know what will ! I think demand will be low for cruises for some time , given the outbreak on the cruise off the coast of Japan and how confined cruises are by their very nature . I ca n't see that many people wanting to spend two weeks in a relatively uncomfortable cabin @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ people . Hopefully demand falling will cut the need for these mega cruise ships , and the desire to make them bigger and more of an eyesore each time . We can but live in hope ! Even before the pandemic , I would have avoided going to Venice because of how crowded by tourists it would be . The same goes for Barcelona , Florence , Amsterdam , Dubrovnik and Paris . I live in London , so it 's not quite the same - I know how to avoid the tourist hotspots and the capital is so big , it 's not so concentrated . But I find the sheer numbers of people in tourist hotspots overwhelming and offputting . I 'd far rather go somewhere that is more off-the-beaten track or authentic . Lisbon and Porto , two cities are really love , were in danger of going the saw way as popular tourist hotspots before the pandemic - although both have retained an authentic sense of Portugal and relatively low costs . But tourists hotbeds hold no appeal to me @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , responsible tourism should n't be beyond us ; the scenes of those huge cruises in the likes of Venice are just horrible , and will hopefully be confined to the past . A few years ago I went to Amsterdam for the day while spending the weekend in Rotterdam - a far more interesting , gritty place , for what its worth . We went to say we 'd been , for the experience , as much as anything . But the novelty factor soon wore off . The area immediately outside the main station was a massive tourist frenzy - as bad as any major tourist hub in London , with tat and tacky shops everywhere you look , and streets so crowded you could n't move . There were overpriced , overcrowded restaurants and bars luring in gullible tourists , who were paying over the odds for cheap beer and shit food . It was ultimately a depressing experience , and a beautiful city - with its canals , architecture and natural charm - had been turned into one big tourist trap ; sanitised , tacky @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ not to mention stag and hen do parties ( another downside of mass tourism ) . Give me somewhere with some character , authenticity and life over that any day of the week . God I loathe tourism . And yet .... as soon as I leave the city I 'm living in , for a look-see somewhere else , there I am with the rest of them . The more I rail at them , in their dull-eyed , selfie-taking millions , the more I 'm also railing at myself . There 's no quality of sensibility or culture that exempts one from the simple rule of equivalency - one extraneous body is the same as another to those whose everyday life has been overwhelmed and negated by the intrusion of tourists . I 've basically given up on travelling . I 'm just grateful that I managed to see so much of the world in my relative youth , in its relative youth ( modernity is ageing the world faster and faster - it 'll essentially be on life support , unconscious and hooked @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ are dead ) . When the time comes , I 'd love to retire somewhere warm and still a graceful expression of its real past - if any such place still exists . But until then I 'll basically just stay put . Do countries need tourism ? What most of them need is the social and economic equality that would let all their people thrive , and live happy , real , empowered lives without tourism . Of course you can have a free market that also controls individual choices . In the USA some national parks such as Yosemite have a limited number of permits . You apply and if you 're lucky then you get a spot . They also limit campsites and hotels and do n't simply keep expanding accommodation to meet demand . This is obviously not really a residential area but similar strategies can be adopted by local councils . I live in a city that gets millions of visitors each year and I have joked that we should build a replica of all the sights about ten miles away @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ atmosphere this spring and a much better one , I feel as if I actually belong here for a change . There have been huge increases in numbers of travellers during my lifetime with vastly cheaper air fares , the end of the Soviet Union and the opening up and new prosperity of China among other factors . While it 's difficult to say that people should n't go abroad given that it 's something I enjoy as well it can put huge pressures on some locations both in prosperous parts of the world and places where it can stress the infrastructure . There have to be some limits whether that 's by increased fares , limited number of permits or rationed flights . Mass tourism us the most profitable form of territorial conquest ever invented . Tourists are economic soldiers who pay to invade as opposed to getting paid to do this . On top of this tourism infrastructure and the maintenance of public places are public costs . Public administrations even use public money to advertise their cities as " theme parks " . Other industrial sectors @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ .

" We want to remember and celebrate his victories , his records and his jubilation . TITLE: Nasdaq Modestly Higher In Mid-Day Trading But Dow Firmly In The Red

CONTENT: Login to your account Nasdaq Modestly Higher In Mid-Day Trading But Dow Firmly In The Red ( RTTNews ) - After ending the previous session on opposite sides of the unchanged line , the major U.S. stock indexes are turning in another mixed performance during trading on Tuesday . The Dow and the S&P 500 have turned negative after seeing initial strength , while the Nasdaq is posting a modest gain . The tech-heavy Nasdaq briefly joined the other major averages in the red in morning trading but is currently up 31.35 points or 0.2 percent at 15,136.93 . Meanwhile , the Dow is down 186.95 points or 0.5 percent at 34,682.68 and the S&P 500 is down 6.86 points or 0.2 percent at 4,461.87 . While the uptick by the Nasdaq comes after the index closed lower for the fourth straight session on Monday , the Dow and the S&P 500 have pulled back after snapping a five-day losing streak in the previous session . Stocks initially benefited from a positive reaction to a highly anticipated Labor Department report showing consumer prices increased by slightly less than expected in the month of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ index rose by 0.3 percent in August after climbing by 0.5 percent in July . Economists had expected consumer prices to increase by 0.4 percent . Excluding food and energy prices , core consumer prices inched up by just 0.1 percent in August after rising by 0.3 percent in July . Economists had been expecting another 0.3 percent increase . The report also showed a slowdown in the annual rate of consumer price growth , which dipped to 5.3 percent in August from 5.4 percent in July . The annual rate of core consumer price growth also slowed to 4.0 percent in August from 4.3 percent in the previous month . The relatively tame inflation data initially generated optimism that the Federal Reserve may delay plans to begin scaling back stimulus . However , subsequent comments from economists have suggested that the Fed is still likely to begin tapering its asset purchases as soon as December . The Fed is scheduled to hold a monetary policy meeting next week , with many expecting the central bank to provide an update on the outlook for @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a significant move to the downside over the course of the session , dragging the KBW Bank Index down by 1.7 percent . Considerable weakness also remains visible among steel stocks , as reflected by the 1.3 percent drop by the NYSE Arca Steel Index . Oil service stocks have also come under pressure as the day has progressed , with the Philadelphia Oil Service Index falling by 1.2 percent . The weakness in the sector comes despite a modest increase by the price of crude oil . Airline , natural gas and tobacco stocks are also seeing notable weakness on the day , while gold stocks have moved higher along with the price of the precious metal . The major European markets also ended the day mixed . While the German DAX Index inched up by 0.1 percent , the French CAC 40 Index and the U.K. 's FTSE 100 Index fell by 0.4 percent and 0.5 percent , respectively . In the bond market , treasuries have shown a strong move to the upside following the consumer price inflation data . Subsequently , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ opposite of its price , is down by 4.5 basis points at 1.279 percent .

TITLE: SCANDAL: Asuogyaman DCE buys a Printer For GH¢16000

CONTENT: The Asuogyaman district assembly in the Eastern region has been hit with a massive price inflation scandal that has angered residents of the district . According to Documents available to the media , prices of some equipment including computers recently purchased by the district Chief executive were heavily inflated . An HP LaserJet coloured printer which was purchased at GHC16,000 by the assembly is sold on the market for GHC1,850 with the same or similar specifications . Also , an HP desktop computer which was bought at GHC4,000 per unit goes at GHC2,500 on the IT market . The staggering price inflation runs through many of the purchases that were done by the DCE and his team . The huge expenditure has infuriated members of the governing New Patriotic Party in the area who have called President Akufo-Adddo to remove of DCE from office . The angry residents have petitioned President Akufo-Addo for his removal from office . A copy of the petition sighted by Starr News said : " Our call is of paramount interest to us due to the fact that since the incumbent DCE assumed office , he has not @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ to keep them abreast always in the news for destroying the good name of the Party and the Presidency that afforded him the opportunity to serve through unimaginable inflated contracts , procurement without recourse to due process and resultant derogatory interactions with the District Finance officer who is committed to protecting the public purse as our intelligence indicates " . The petition further said : " Conclusively , we strongly believe in decentralized governance and equitable distribution of authority and believe his timely removal from office and investigation for acts of corruption will serve as a unifying front of your able administration which will enhance your relationship with the constituents and ensure development through reputable , responsible and accountable " . When contacted by the Starr News on the claims Tuesday , Mr Kwame Agyekum declined to comment but noted the Economic and Organised Crime Office ( EOCO ) is investigating the issue .

We also may change the frequency you receive our emails from us in order to keep you up to date and give you the best relevant information possible . As always you can unsubscribe at any time . More info ? Group 28 Thank you for subscribing ! A Jet2 holiday flight to Gran Canaria was forced to turn around and return to Manchester this evening . The Boeing 757-200 took off from Manchester Airport at 6.41pm but a few minutes into the flight the captain decided to abort the trip and instead flew west over Wales where it maintained a holding pattern before returning to Manchester . The jet cruised at around 16,000ft over the west coast of Wales before returning to Manchester , more than two hours after it had taken off . It is standard procedure for aircraft to burn fuel to achieve the correct weight before any @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , the airline reported that the plane had to return ' due to unforeseen operational reasons ' . Jet2 flight LS781 from Manchester to Gran Canaria was forced to return to Manchester Airport due to ' unforseen operational reasons ' ( Image : flightradar24.com ) When the M.E.N . asked the airline for a comment , a spokeswoman said : " We can confirm that flight LS781 followed standard procedure and returned to Manchester Airport after the crew reported a technical indication . " The spokeswoman confirmed that customers would be flown to Gran Canaria on a replacement aircraft .

The legendary Ireland goalkeeper played in 60 derbies for the Glasgow giants , a number only Billy McNeill , Paul McStay and Roy Aitken could beat . This is the first for Ireland skipper Duffy , and Bonner admits it 's a shame the Derry man wo n't get to experience the atmosphere he relished so much over his 16 year Hoops career . " Listen , the experience is incredible when it 's a full house , at home especially in front of your own @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . " Everything about it is special and not to have that is probably taking away a bit for Shane . " But it 's still a huge game , a game everyone is watching so I think he 'll enjoy the game even in these circumstances . " Duffy will experience something Bonner never did when playing in an empty ground -- but the fomer Ireland No.1 had his own unique occasion in 1994 when he played at Ibrox with only Rangers fans allowed in . Packie Bonner says Ireland 's young players should get the chance to shine " Going to Ibrox was always a bit of a challenge , but I always loved it -- in fact sometimes I preferred playing there instead of Parkhead because the expectations were low , " said the Hoops legend . " We had to go into the Lions den , and if you did well there was no better place to turn them over . URL: http://www.baystreet.ca/articles/marketwatch.aspx?id=1578 COUNTRY: ca CONTINENT: America TITLE: Baystreet.ca - Biotechs Offer a Glimmer of Hope Amid the ... CONTENT: Biotechs Offer a Glimmer of Hope Amid the Deadly COVID-19 Outbreak The deadly coronavirus is having a serious economic impact on the world and causing widespread panic . Major stock markets saw a much-needed uptick on Thursday , but it 's uncertain how long that will last . The Nasdaq , S&P 500 and the Dow Jones were all in the green on Thursday after weeks of red , offering investors some respite after Wednesday 's sharp losses . Despite all of the doom and gloom , biotech stocks have remained stable and some have even seen their share prices increase as the world searches for a viable cure for the coronavirus or a vaccine to prevent further spread . Some biotech companies like CoCrystal Pharma , Inc . ( NASDAQ:COCP ) and Gilead Sciences ( NASDAQ:GILD ) are working around the clock to develop a treatment , while others like Moderna Inc . ( NASDAQ:MRNA ) and Inovio Pharmaceuticals ( NASDAQ:INO ) are focused on creating coronavirus vaccines . Then , there are companies like Rockwell Medical ( NASDAQ:RMTI ) who treat high-risk kidney disease patients that could suffer the most from this deadly disease . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ has clocked in a death toll of nearly 10,000 , could reach $2 trillion unless countries around the world take urgent steps . Some precautions have already been taken , such as shutting down large public venues , postponing or cancelling large events , and even implementing citywide lockdowns . Now , everyone is looking to biotech companies to develop a vaccine or a treatment for the virus . Biotech Companies Race to Protect the Public from Coronavirus There has been serious headway on both ends , with over 300 separate trials into different drugs and therapies being launched around the world . Last week , CoCrystal Pharma , Inc . ( NASDAQ:COCP ) announced that it is pursuing the development of novel antiviral compounds for the treatment of coronavirus infections after signing a licensing agreement with Kansas State University Research Foundation . The company has also closed two rounds of financing in the last month , one at the end of February for US$11 million and a second on March 11 for US$6.8 million . Meanwhile , Moderna Inc. ' s ( NASDAQ:MRNA ) antiviral medicine remdesivir @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ move into Phase 1 human trials on 45 healthy adult volunteers in the coming weeks . Of course , vaccines and treatments are likely months away from hitting the market due to the nature in which new drugs must be tested . In the meantime , biotech companies like Rockwell Medical ( NASDAQ:RMTI ) are helping keep diabetes patients who are at higher risk of dying from the coronavirus . Rockwell Medical has developed its drug Triferic to significantly improve iron and hemoglobin levels in kidney disease patients who are undergoing hemodialysis . The company 's Dialysate Triferic launched to the US market in May 2019 , while it 's intravenous ( IV ) Triferic is up for PDUFA review on March 28 . Triferic is the first and only FDA-approved product indicated to maintain hemoglobin in HD-dependent chronic kidney disease patients . Rockwell Medical ( NASDAQ:RMTI ) just announced a US$35 million debt financing agreement with Innovatus Capital Partners , which will provide the necessary capital to further advance its treatments . While treating the coronavirus directly is very important at a time like this , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ in the meantime . At present , the majority of the people who have died from the virus are 60 years of age or older , with the largest group of fatalities being over 80 years old . This demographic is also the most likely to develop kidney disease and in turn , have a greater risk of dying from coronavirus . In fact , kidney involvement has been frequent in patients who have tested positive for coronavirus and could be a predictor for mortality . Kidney disease is one of the most common diseases worldwide , affecting over 850 million people across the globe . Roughly 37 million US adults have chronic kidney disease , with the largest percentage ( 38% ) being 65 or older . Apart from a kidney transplant , the only way to treat individuals with kidney disease is through hemodialysis , which uses a machine to filter waste and excess fluids from your blood when your kidneys ca n't . Unfortunately , hemodialysis can also result in blood loss and iron sequestration leading to anemia and can severely lower a patient 's immune system @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the Coronavirus Outbreak People around the world are hopeful that a treatment or vaccine for the coronavirus will be announced in the coming weeks and although it may not reach the market that fast , things are looking up . As mentioned , several biotech companies have made some serious headway in the development of both vaccines and treatments for COVID-19 after the Chinese authorities shared the genetic sequence of the virus on January 11 . At the end of February , Gilead Sciences ( NASDAQ:GILD ) initiated two Phase 3 clinical studies to evaluate the safety and efficacy of its drug remdesivir in adults diagnosed with coronavirus . The randomized , open-label studies will enrol around 1,000 patients at medical centers in Asian countries and other regions with a high number of diagnosed cases . CoCrystal Pharma , Inc . ( NASDAQ:COCP ) has also ramped up its efforts to develop a treatment for COVID-19 thanks to a license agreement with KSURF to further develop certain proprietary broad-spectrum antiviral compounds to treat norovirus and coronavirus infections . The company said it intends to pursue research and development @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . When it comes to vaccines , many believe Moderna Inc . ( NASDAQ:MRNA ) offers one of the most viable options now that the company has commenced the first testing in humans for its mRNA-1273 vaccine . The company initially developed the vaccine to fight against SARS-CoV-2 , so it was already ahead of the game in its development . Inovio Pharmaceuticals ( NASDAQ:INO ) also accelerated its timeline for developing its DNA vaccine INO-4800 to address COVID-19 and will be commencing human clinical trials in April on 30 healthy volunteers in the US . Of course , until a viable vaccine is mass-produced , which will likely take at least a year , countries around the world will continue to take precautionary measures to reduce the spread of COVID-19 . For individuals this means canceling travel plans , practicing social distancing , and staying at home as much as possible . The global economic impact of the coronavirus remains to be seen , but it 's clear that these biotech companies offer a bright spot in an otherwise dire situation . TITLE: Marine pollution: NGT fines Qatari shipping firm Rs 100 cr, Adani Rs ...

CONTENT: the tribunal to impose environment compensation on those it finds guilty of damaging the environment . This is known as the " polluter pays principle " . In this case , the NGT also invoked the " principle of strict liability " after finding that the ship was running in a poor condition . " Respondents are defaulting entities which have not complied with law and have adopted a most careless and reckless attitude in relation to protecting the marine environment , " the judgment pronounced on 23 August by the NGT 's principal bench read . The 4-member bench is headed by NGT chairperson Justice Swatanter Kumar . " MV Rak was carrying 60,000 tonnes of coal for Adani , 290 tonnes of fuel oil , 50 tonnes of diesel when it sank " MV Rak was registered in Panama but owned by Delta International , a Qatari company . It sank 20 nautical miles or 37 km off Mumbai 's coast on 4 August 2011 . Apart from coal , it was carrying 290 tonnes of fuel oil and 50 tonnes of diesel .

URL: https://thesun.ng/updated-tinubu-seeks-senate-approval-for-fresh-n1-7-trillion-external-loan/
COUNTRY: ng
CONTINENT: Africa

TITLE: UPDATED: Tinubu seeks Senate approval for fresh N1.7 trillion external loan

CONTENT: President Tinubu , in a letter addressed to the National Assembly on Tuesday , requested the approval of a fresh N1.767 trillion as a new external borrowing plan in the 2024 appropriation act . Tinubu explained in his request that the loan is based on an exchange rate of N800 to $1 , adding that the request was part of Nigeria 's budgetary financing plan aimed at addressing a portion of the ? 9.17 trillion fiscal deficit in the 2024 budget . Tinubu 's request was read by Senate President Godswill Akpabio . Accordingly , if approved , the loan will be used to part-finance the budget deficit of N9.7 trillion for the 2024 budget . Tinubu also added that the amount of $2.209 billion ( N1.767 trillion ) will be raised through Eurobonds or other external borrowing instruments . The letter also provided detailed terms and conditions for the issuance of Eurobonds in the international capital market to raise the required sum . The president has also submitted the MTEF/FSP 2025-2027 to the Senate and the National Social Investment Programme establishment amendment bill @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ implementation of the federal government 's social welfare programmes . The implication of Tinubu 's request to obtain an additional $2.2 billion fresh borrowing plan means that Nigeria 's external debt may increase to $45.1 billion by the end of 2024 . The MTEF/FSP request , which seeks expeditious passage , was referred to Senator Sani Musa , APC , Niger East-led Senate Committees on Finance and National and Economic Planning to report back as soon as practicable . This is as the Central Bank of Nigeria ( CBN ) recently said the Federal Government spent $3.58 billion servicing the country 's foreign debt in the first nine months of 2024 . CBN 's data on international payment statistics showed that the amount represents a 39.77 per cent increase from the $2.56 billion spent during the same period in 2023 . According to the report , while the highest monthly debt servicing payment in 2024 occurred in May , amounting to $854.37 million , the highest monthly expenditure in 2023 was $641.70 million , recorded in July . External debt for the states and @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ $4.89 billion within the period under review . The letter also provided detailed terms and conditions for the issuance of Eurobonds in the international capital market to raise the required sum . Accordingly , Tinubu directed the Honourable Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy , alongside the DMO , to take all necessary steps to execute the plan upon National Assembly approval . In another letter , the President asked the Senate for an amendment to the National Social Investment Agency Act to make the social register the main source of data for all investment programmes .

Their faces looked almost pained as they attempted to pull some kind of expression on their heavily Botoxed faces and there was n't one raised eyebrow or creased forehead amongst them . Not so scary now : Randomly , LaToya Jackson was also at the premiere , looking , for her , almost normal ' It 's such eye candy , " Bravo 's senior vice president for original programming and development , Andy Cohen tells the New York Times .
